hero.tagline: The definitive archive of Phasmatodea in Mexico and Latin America

phasmids.intro_body: Phasmids (order Phasmatodea) are herbivorous insects known for their extraordinary mimicry of sticks, leaves and bark. With over 3,500 described species worldwide, Mexico harbors an exceptional diversity of forms, sizes and behaviors still poorly documented.
phasmids.intro_body_2: Phasma MX was created as a scientific reference archive for biologists, enthusiasts and conservationists, with emphasis on native fauna from Mexico, Central America and exotic species of taxonomic interest.
phasmids.pull_quote: Phasmids are the result of millions of years of co-evolution with the plants around them — living architecture.

catalog.label: Phasmatodea · Systematic catalog
catalog.description: Systematic registry of phasmids with taxonomic sheets, field photographs and bibliographic references. Includes native Mexican species, exotic species of scientific interest and endemic taxa.

filters.no_results_desc: No species found with the selected filters.

catalog.contribute_body: If you have field photographs, distribution data or publications on phasmids in Mexico or Latin America, contact us to collaborate.

species_detail.food_plants_note: List based on captive breeding data. Plants listed with scientific name are preferred in the field.
species_detail.no_related: No other species of this family in the catalog.

articles_page.label: Phasma MX · Publications and outreach
articles_page.description: Field expeditions, taxonomic reviews, species profiles and breeding guides. A window into the scientific work and outreach on Phasmatodea in Mexico and Latin America.

about.hero_body: Phasma MX was born from a simple conviction: that Mexico's phasmids deserve a systematic, rigorous and accessible registry. We are a small team of biologists, editors and enthusiasts who document, publish and communicate knowledge about the order Phasmatodea in Mexico and Latin America.
about.mission_body_1: To build the most complete archive of Phasmatodea in Mexico: with verified taxonomic sheets, field photographs, georeferenced distribution, and articles that translate scientific research for broad audiences.
about.mission_body_2: We are not an academic institution or a company. We are a collaborative project open to biologists, photographers, breeders and anyone who wants to contribute to the knowledge of these extraordinary insects.
about.principle_1_desc: All catalog content is verified against primary sources and the Phasmida Species File Online.
about.principle_2_desc: The archive is free to access. Distribution data is downloadable for scientific use.
about.principle_3_desc: When something is unknown, we say so. We distinguish between taxonomic certainty and preliminary records.
about.collaborate_title: The archive grows with every record
about.collaborate_body: If you have field photographs, distribution data, breeding records or publications on phasmids in Mexico or Latin America, we want to hear from you. You don't need to be a researcher: any documented observation has scientific value.

not_found.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. Perhaps the specimen was reclassified, or it is simply pending documentation.
